mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and raises the present value of equity cash flows; when the 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS is negative, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if the 3-month FEDFUNDS decline signal produces nonpositive mean 20-day S&P 500 returns or Rank IC below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses into severe recession territory, expected earnings downgrades dominate valuation support; a crossing above 9.0 percent predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of cases or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero while UNRATE is extremely high, investors price continued policy accommodation and a policy put; the joint condition predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5 percent and UNRATE above 8.0 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE condition does not deliver higher 60-day returns than either single-series condition, or if triggered mean returns are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in RSAFS signals improving household demand and near-term revenue growth; a rising 3-month trend in retail sales predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive average 20-day returns or if its directional hit rate is below 50 percent in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO below a recessionary threshold signals factory contraction and weak operating leverage; when INDPRO crosses below 96, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO-below-96 triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of cases or have nonnegative average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Very low HOUST indicates depressed residential investment and weak credit-sensitive demand; when housing starts fall below 600 thousand units, S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days are negative.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-600 episodes have 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional mean or if the negative coefficient is not stable across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS pinned below 0.5 percent signals reflation under constrained nominal policy rates; the resulting fall in expected real rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises over 6 months and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent
regime_hint: post-shock reflation at the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-below-0.5 condition has lower 60-day returns than CPIAUCSL momentum alone or produces negative mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in PAYEMS and INDPRO confirm synchronized labor and production weakness; this interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and INDPRO both decline month over month
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous PAYEMS and INDPRO declines are not followed by negative 5-day returns on average or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus separate effects.
